title: Worked Example - Using RequestBody for Input

content.problemStatement:
**Step 1: Run the Application** \
Start your Spring Boot application \
Once the application runs successfully, the API Tester page will open automatically in your browser

---

**Step 2: Test “Submit Feedback” POST API** \
In the API tester:
- Select **POST** as the **HTTP** method
- In the URL input field, type:
```sss
/feedback
```
In the request body section, enter JSON:
```json
{
  "username": "alice",
  "message": "Very easy to understand API example"
}
```
Click the `Send` button

**What happens:**
- The POST request is sent to the backend
- The feedback data is sent in **JSON format**
- `@RequestBody` reads the JSON data from the request body
- Spring converts the JSON into a `Feedback` Java object
- The feedback object is added to the in-memory list
- A success message is returned

---


**Step 3: Test “Get All Feedback” GET API** \
In the API tester:
- Select **GET** as the HTTP method
- In the URL input field, type:
```sss
/feedback
```
Click the `Send` button

**What happens:**
- The **GET** request is sent to the backend
- The controller fetches all feedback from the list
- The stored feedback is returned as a response

---

**For Testing:**

After sending your **POST** request, you can select **GET** and type **`/feedback`** in your API tester to check whether your feedback has been added successfully.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
